name: Bhai Devinder Singh Gurdaspuri
body: Bhai Devinder Singh Ji was born in the village of Daulat Chak belonging to the Shakargarh township of district Gurdaspur. Shakargarh was the only township of Gurdaspur to be included in Pakistan at the time of partition in 1947. Bhai Devinder Singh Ji was born into the home of a renowned Raagi, Bhai Mehtab Singh on the 10th of December 1929. Bhai Sahib’s mother was the daughter of a very famous poet, Giani Kartar Singh ‘Classwaleya’, who had also served for some time as head ‘Granthi’ in Sri Harmandir Sahib, Amritsar. With such strong roots in music and Sikh history, it was only natural that Bhai Devinder Singh Ji got inclined towards Kirtan. His initial training in music began at home with his father but he was also greatly influenced by Ustad Sohan Singh Ji ‘Padam Shri’.

Following the partition in 1947, their family relocated permanently to the village of Nabipur, which was located right next to Gurdaspur. With expansion over time, it is now a part of Gurdaspur. By this time, Bhai Devinder Singh Ji was already a very good singer, but he had the desire to learn more and improve on his art. He became a student of Master Jagdish Raj Kohli, who himself was a ‘shagird’ of the renowned Pandit Vishnu Digambar Paluskar. Master Jagdish Raj Ji taught Bhai Devinder Singh Ji various forms of classical and semi-classical music including Khyal, Thumri, Dadra and Kajri.  With the knowledge received, he practiced rigorously day and night to master all these forms of music.  In 1962, he also went on to attain a Master of Arts (MA) degree in music from the Prayag Sangeet Samiti located in Allahabad.

Bhai Devinder Singh Ji was a very popular ‘lok geet’ singer and due to his achievements, he was appointed as a producer with All India Radio. Over time, he performed regularly as a radio artist in Jalandhar, Banares, Jammu and Allahabad. He has performed with artists such as Bibi Surinder Kaur, Bibi Prakash Kaur, Bibi Ajit Kaur and Shashi Bala. Some of his popular tracks include ‘Ay Punjab Kara Ki Sifat Teri’, ‘Aades Tise Aades’, ‘Miteya Andhera Chand Chadeya’, ‘Deh Shiva Bar Mohe Ehai’ and ‘Soora So Pehchaniye’. In his book, ‘Gurmat Sangeet De Anmol Ratan’, Bhai Nirmal Singh Khalsa noted that until 1972, Bhai Devinder Singh Ji only sang ‘lok geet’. However, in one of his recordings, Bhai Devinder Singh Ji mentioned that he started performing Kirtan in 1970. It was around this time that Bhai Devinder Singh Ji met Sant Sadhu Singh Ji from Nanaksar, who advised him to start doing Kirtan in the Sewa of our Gurus instead of singing worldly songs. He took Sant Sandhu Singh Ji’s advice to heart and started to perform Kirtan with his brothers. His ‘jatha’ included Bhai Onkar Singh on the side harmonium and Bhai Jograj Singh on tabla.

He also had a strong presence in ‘Shastri Sangeet’. In 1966, he won first prize in a Sangeet Samelan held in Lucknow. Many notable artists such as Pandit Bhimshen Joshi Ji took part in this Samelan. In honor of his service in the field of music, he was bestowed with the Shiromani Award by the government of Punjab in 1968. Bhai Devinder Singh Ji traveled abroad to many countries including Canada, America, England, Malaysia and more. Most of the recordings below are from these tours. He was also the first Kirtaniya to be featured on BBC London.

Bhai Devinder Singh Ji was in his peak when his life was cut short due to some misguided individuals. It was his daily routine to walk about in his fields every evening. Just like any other day, he set out for his walk and on his way home, he was attacked and shot by someone waiting for him in the bushes. Bhai Devinder Singh Ji left for his heavenly abode at 7.35 p.m. on the 25th of May 1987. The information above was obtained from ‘Punjab De Parsidh Raagi Rababi’ by Sardar Balbir Singh Kanwal and ‘Gurmat Sangeet De Anmol Ratan’ by Bhai Nirmal Singh Khalsa. Photo courtesy of Neelu Mann. If anyone has more recordings or photos of Bhai Devinder Singh Ji to share, please contact us.
